focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Rev. Sullivan warns Landon to stay away from his house and Jamie, asserting control, but Jamie arrives cheerfully with lemonade, unaware of the tension.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Father warning Landon to stay away from his daughter and home
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Rev. Sullivan directly tells Landon to keep away from his house and Jamie after observing him in silence
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Prevent Landon from interacting with Jamie or entering his house
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Landon has been warned, and Jamie arrives unaware of the tension
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Landon will heed the warning
evidence_quotes: The Reverend waits, letting Landon suffer the silence.
His eyes lock with Landon's.

relation_updates: Makes adversarial stance toward Landon explicit
status_updates: Issues direct warning to Landon
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Protective and authoritative father

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jamie argues with her father, Rev. Sullivan, about befriending the Carter boy, who accidentally caused Harold Keene's death. He restricts her from seeing him outside school, prompting her assertion of independence.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Father restricting daughter's friendship due to safety concerns
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Rev. Sullivan tells Jamie not to see the Carter boy outside school, citing his recklessness in the accident, and Jamie asserts her need for independence.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Jamie accepts the restriction but declares her autonomy.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Tension over Jamie's freedom to choose friends
evidence_quotes: REV. SULLIVAN I don't want you to see him outside school activities.
JAMIE Fine. But I need to start deciding how to spend my time and my life.
REV. SULLIVAN Jamie, he's careless. Reckless.

relation_updates: conflict with Jamie over her friendship with the Carter boy

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Landon asks Reverend Sullivan for permission to take Jamie to dinner on New Year's Eve, expresses his care for her, and promises not to hurt her; Sullivan agrees with conditions.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: protective father negotiating with suitor
timeline_nodes.salient_development: After Landon apologizes for past treatment and promises not to hurt Jamie, Reverend Sullivan agrees to let him take her to dinner on New Year's Eve with conditions.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: He has permitted Landon to take Jamie to dinner on New Year's Eve, with the conditions that she is home by one and they drive carefully.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Landon will keep his promise and not hurt Jamie.
evidence_quotes: REV. SULLIVAN That won't be possible.
REV. SULLIVAN I don't want to see her hurt.
REV. SULLIVAN Have her home by one. And drive carefully.

relation_updates: Trust established with Landon Carter, allowing him to take Jamie out
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Protective father concerned for Jamie's safety and well-being.

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 148、INT. PARSONAGE - SULLIVAN KITCHEN - LATER THAT NIGHT
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Reverend Sullivan questions Jamie's relationship with Landon, appealing to her insecurities; though defensive, Jamie hesitates, acknowledging the validity of his concern about trust and truth.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: father confronting daughter about her relationship with Landon
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Urges Jamie to tell Landon about her illness by questioning if she can trust him.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Have Jamie disclose her illness to Landon
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Jamie acknowledges that telling Landon is the right thing to do
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Jamie has not yet told Landon about her illness
evidence_quotes: It's time to tell him. (pause) It would be the right thing.
You're sure? You're sure you can trust him?
Isn't it better to know?
Their eyes meet. She knows he's right.

relation_updates: Confronts Jamie about her relationship with Landon
Jamie accuses him of wanting her all to himself

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Landon confronts Reverend Sullivan about a terminal illness, questioning faith and prayer as he struggles with the injustice of the situation.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: father and reverend defending faith against Landon's challenge
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Reverend Sullivan tells Landon that he and Jamie are still praying for a miracle after Landon accuses him of giving up on her terminal illness.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Landon leaves the kitchen in anger before Reverend Sullivan can finish his explanation.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Landon's anger and questioning of God over Jamie's illness remain unresolved.
evidence_quotes: Her doctors have. Jamie and I. We're still praying for a miracle.
Two, three months. Maybe less. Maybe more.

relation_updates: Landon confronts him about Jamie's prognosis
Defends his faith to Landon's skepticism
status_updates: His faith is directly challenged by Landon
auxiliary.persona_anchor: a faithful father maintaining hope through prayer

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jamie, heartbroken and angry, confronts her father about Landon and her illness, expressing rage at him, Landon, and God, then flees upstairs to the attic.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Father and pastor attempting to manage Jamie's anger and crisis of faith
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Confronts Jamie's anger at him and God, tries to redirect it to Landon, and offers pastoral reassurance about God accepting anger.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To comfort Jamie and guide her anger away from himself through religious counsel
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Jamie rejects his comfort and flees to the attic, remaining angry and unresolved
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Jamie's anger at God and her illness persists without resolution
evidence_quotes: REV. SULLIVAN I wanted to think he'd changed.
REV. SULLIVAN Jamie, you're not mad at me. You're mad at Landon --
REV. SULLIVAN That's normal. God accepts your anger. He won't punish you.

relation_updates: Jamie expresses anger at him and God
Expresses disappointment in Landon's insufficient change
auxiliary.persona_anchor: A father providing pastoral care in a personal crisis

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Landon offers help to Reverend Sullivan and Jamie, suggesting to drive her to school, but Jamie refuses, appears unwell, and declares she doesn't care who knows, leaving both men uneasy.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Father and reverend present when Jamie declares she is quitting school
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Witnesses Jamie refuse to return to school, appearing pale and unwell, and shares an uncomfortable look with Landon acknowledging her decline
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To maintain Jamie's school attendance, as implied by Landon's offer to drive her
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Both men are uneasy and concerned after Jamie's uncharacteristic statement
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Jamie's worsening health and withdrawal from normal life
evidence_quotes: Reverend Sullivan is up a ladder, cleaning a gutter.
the Reverend is busy ignoring Landon standing below.
The Reverend looks down.
Landon and Reverend Sullivan look at each other, then away, uncomfortable. That didn't sound like her.

relation_updates: Shares uncomfortable understanding with Landon about Jamie's condition
Initially ignores Landon's help offer
status_updates: Jamie refuses to return to school

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Reverend Sullivan admits he was wrong about God taking Jamie's mother, promises to always be with her, and they share a heartfelt embrace.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Father admitting error and offering comfort
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Tells Jamie he was wrong to say God wanted her mother more and promises to always be with her.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Jamie embraces him tightly, tearful and unwilling to let go.
evidence_quotes: I was wrong. Nobody could have wanted or loved your mother more than we did. Not even God.
I will always be with you.

relation_updates: Strengthens bond with Jamie through apology and commitment
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Father who corrects past mistake and commits to presence.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Landon and Reverend Sullivan share a moment of vulnerability; the Reverend reveals his past, expresses liking for Landon, and they shake hands as allies.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Reverend who discloses his juvenile imprisonment to Landon and forms an alliance.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Reveals his past imprisonment and shakes hands with Landon, becoming allies.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Allies with Landon after sharing his past.
evidence_quotes: The Reverend is wiping his glasses, his face, maybe of tears.
REV. SULLIVAN When I was seventeen, I was sent up for a year for stealing a car.
Sometimes I still feel that boy inside me and I don't like him. (beat) But I like you.
The two men shake. Allies now.

relation_updates: becomes allies with Landon
discloses past to Landon
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Vulnerable mentor who admits past mistakes and expresses liking for Landon.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Landon, moved by Reverend Sullivan's words that Jamie's miracle was him, embraces the reverend, finding closure as he prepares to remarry, honoring Jamie's wish.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Father of the bride officiating Jamie's wedding; elderly man in later reunion with Landon
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Officiates Jamie and Landon's wedding by blessing them and reading a passage, and years later, frail and elderly, has an awkward reunion with Landon
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Awaiting Landon's explanation in an uncertain conversation
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The reason for Landon's visit is unclear; their relationship is now distant
evidence_quotes: Reverend Sullivan kneels, too.
Reverend Sullivan. He's stooped, thin, balding. His skin parchment thin.
The two men embrace. Stiff, uncomfortable, uncertain.

relation_updates: officiates daughter's wedding
reunites awkwardly with Landon years later
status_updates: ages and becomes frail

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Reverend Sullivan starts as a protective father distrustful of Landon Carter, setting strict boundaries to control Jamie's interactions. Over time, he witnesses Landon's sincerity and Jamie's growing independence, leading to a gradual shift in his attitude as he confronts her illness and his own faith. Ultimately, he reconciles with Jamie, forms an alliance with Landon, and officiates their wedding, reflecting his personal growth and acceptance.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Landon sees Jamie on the fence with a mirror reflecting headlights; they exchange a look as Belinda's voice comes through the CB radio.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Jamie reflects headlights with a mirror, drawing Landon's attention.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Jamie's reflection of headlights causes Landon's annoyance to turn to mild respect.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: They stare at each other, with Landon feeling mild respect.
evidence_quotes: LANDON'S POV Jamie sitting atop, holding a large mirror, reflecting the headlights back into the car.
They stare at each other.
His annoyance turns to mild respect.

relation_updates: Landon's perception shifts from annoyance to mild respect toward Jamie
status_updates: Positioned on a fence holding a mirror
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Jamie is on a fence holding a mirror.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jamie and Landon argue while driving, with Jamie revealing a rule list where 42 means befriend an enemy, implying Landon is one, as he worries about consequences affecting his school play role.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Jamie states that rule 42 on her life list is to befriend an enemy and identifies Landon as that enemy.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Jamie explicitly tells Landon he is an enemy she is befriending according to her life list rule 42.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To befriend an enemy as part of her life list.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Landon admits he needs help with the school play to avoid expulsion, and Jamie continues driving with him.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Jamie's complete life list and other rules remain undisclosed.
evidence_quotes: 42 is befriend an enemy.
Kinda. Yeah.
Yeah. Kelly'll make me leave school if I screw up the play.

relation_updates: Defines Landon as an enemy, shifting relationship dynamic
status_updates: Drives dangerously on the wrong side of the road
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Structured and goal-driven, following a personal rule list.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jamie and Landon exchange Rainmaker scripts at the cemetery, affirming mutual trust as their conversation on faith and doubt continues.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Jamie reveals her mother's burial location and shares her telescope with Landon
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Jamie discloses that her mother is buried nearby, shows her homemade telescope, and expresses hope of seeing her mother again while discussing astronomy with Landon
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Jamie and Landon share a quiet moment of connection after discussing personal loss and astronomy
evidence_quotes: JAMIE My mother's buried near here.
JAMIE I hope so. (beat) I think maybe she sees me now.
JAMIE (re: telescope) I'm building a larger one to see the nucleus of Haley's Comet --

relation_updates: Bonds with Landon over shared personal losses and interests, affirming mutual trust
status_updates: Discloses mother's burial location and owns a homemade telescope
auxiliary.persona_anchor: faith-driven and astronomically interested

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Landon and Jamie share a heartfelt, improvised moment on stage that feels real, leading to a genuine kiss; the audience reacts variably, while Belinda appears upset as the act ends.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Jamie, playing Lizzie in the school play, shares an improvised kiss with co-star Landon.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: During the play, Jamie and Landon share a genuine kiss on stage in front of the audience.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: After the kiss, Jamie has tears in her eyes and avoids Landon's gaze by looking at the floor.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The kiss's authenticity and its implications for their real relationship are unclear.
evidence_quotes: From stage right, Jamie makes her entrance as Lizzie. A collective gasp from the audience.
Landon can't take his eyes off her.
They kiss. Gently, genuinely.
Landon tries to meet Jamie's gaze. But she's looking at the floor.

relation_updates: Romantic connection with Landon becomes public
Relationship with Belinda becomes adversarial
status_updates: Transforms into a captivating presence on stage
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Emotionally open and sincere in her performance on stage.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Landon reveals a star named after Jamie, who reciprocates his love; they share a passionate kiss, then discuss marriage amid blooming dogwoods, with Landon respecting her boundaries.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Jamie tells Landon she loves him and expresses a wish to marry in spring
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Jamie first says 'I love you' to Landon and later shyly reveals her dream of getting married in April when the dogwoods bloom
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To marry Landon in April when the dogwoods bloom
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Jamie and Landon have shared declarations of love and a future marriage aspiration
evidence_quotes: JAMIE It's wonderful. I love you.
JAMIE (very shy) In April when the dogwoods and wisteria are blooming... Get married.

relation_updates: Declares love for Landon
Proposes marriage to Landon
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Hopeful and romantically committed

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 152、EXT. WOODS BY SIDE OF ROAD - MOMENTS LATER
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jamie explains she withheld her leukemia diagnosis to avoid change, especially with Landon, whom she sees as both a gift and a cruel joke from God, then leaves him stunned.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Jamie tells Landon she has leukemia.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Jamie reveals her leukemia diagnosis, explains she kept it secret to avoid people acting weird around her, and states that Landon is both a gift and a cruel joke from God.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Jamie has disclosed her illness and left Landon standing stunned by the road.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Landon's response to learning about Jamie's illness.
evidence_quotes: JAMIE I found out last winter. I'm not responding to treatment anymore.
JAMIE The doctors said to do everything the same as long as possible. (pause) I didn't want anyone being -- weird around me.
JAMIE Especially you. (long beat) God gives me an illness, then you. A cruel joke.

relation_updates: Landon learns of Jamie's illness
Jamie expresses mixed feelings about Landon as both a gift and cruel joke
status_updates: Health status revealed as terminal leukemia
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Jamie is terminally ill with leukemia and describes Landon as part of a cruel joke.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jamie, heartbroken and angry, confronts her father about Landon and her illness, expressing rage at him, Landon, and God, then flees upstairs to the attic.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Jamie confronts her father during dinner about Landon and her illness.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Jamie expresses furious anger at her father, Landon, the universe, and God, and states that her illness is a punishment from God.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Flees to the attic to isolate herself.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Confusion and anger over why God is punishing her with illness.
evidence_quotes: JAMIE (furious) I am mad at you! And at Landon! And the universe! And God!
JAMIE By making me ill, he is punishing me! (quieter) I just don't know what for.
She flees the room.
Up another flight to the attic.

relation_updates: Relationship with father becomes confrontational
status_updates: Emotional state shifts to furious and overwhelmed
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Furious and questioning faith.

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 173、INT. BEAUFORT HIGH - CORRIDOR - HALF HOUR LATER
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jamie reveals her fear and loneliness, feeling abandoned by faith; Landon comforts her and promises to help her live until she dies, reaffirming his support.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Jamie discloses her fears and receives Landon's promise of support
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Jamie admits she is always frightened and feels alone, leading Landon to promise to help her live until she dies
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Jamie has Landon's explicit commitment to support her remaining life
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Jamie's fear and terminal illness are ongoing
evidence_quotes: JAMIE: All the time.
JAMIE: Help me live until I die?
LANDON: I will.

relation_updates: Landon commits to supporting Jamie emotionally and practically
status_updates: Jamie's emotional vulnerability is exposed
Faith crisis is acknowledged
auxiliary.persona_anchor: fearful but accepting of support

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jamie is in bed, connected to medical equipment, with Landon holding her hand as Reverend Sullivan receives bad news from a doctor.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Unconscious patient in hospital bed
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Jamie is unconscious in a hospital bed, hooked to monitors, pumps, and an IV drip.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Unconscious and connected to medical equipment in hospital bed.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Doctor is delivering bad news about her health status.
evidence_quotes: Jamie lies in bed, eyes closed.
Hooked to monitors, pumps, an IV drip.

relation_updates: Landon holds her hand at bedside, showing his commitment
status_updates: Hospitalized in critical condition
Unconscious and on life support
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Unconscious and dependent on life support equipment.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jamie gives Landon a book her mother made, containing quotes and thoughts; they reflect on love and faith, and Jamie expresses her desire to go home, acknowledging the challenges it entails.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Hospitalized patient sharing a personal book from her mother
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Jamie gives Landon a book of quotes made by her mother, states that her anger has passed, and expresses a wish to go home, noting the cost involved.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To return home from the hospital
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Has accepted her illness but identifies financial barriers to returning home
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Cost of home care
evidence_quotes: JAMIE I have something for you. She reaches under her pillow for the worn book from the attic. She offers it to Landon.
JAMIE (looking away) Better. I was really angry. LANDON I know. JAMIE It's gone now.
JAMIE (almost whispering) I want to go home.
JAMIE (looking away) It's not that simple. (pause) It costs money to do this at home.

relation_updates: Deepens intimacy by sharing mother's book with Landon
status_updates: Shifts from anger to spiritual acceptance of illness
auxiliary.persona_anchor: accepting and sharing a family legacy

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Landon, moved by Reverend Sullivan's words that Jamie's miracle was him, embraces the reverend, finding closure as he prepares to remarry, honoring Jamie's wish.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Bride in wedding ceremony to Landon Carter
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Jamie walks down the aisle with her father, stops to rest due to exhaustion, and completes the wedding ceremony while seated in a wheelchair.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Married to Landon Carter, physically exhausted and seated in a wheelchair at the altar.
evidence_quotes: LANDON'S POV - JAMIE Flanked by a nurse and Reverend Sullivan. She's wearing her Lizzie dress from the play.
Jamie -- halfway -- stops to rest and catch her breath.
Jamie sits. Exhausted.

relation_updates: Married to Landon Carter
Father-daughter bond reaffirmed during ceremony
status_updates: Became married
Physically exhausted requiring wheelchair
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Bride who is visibly ill but smiling and participating in the ceremony.

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Jamie Sullivan starts as a principled, isolated student who forms a complex relationship with Landon Carter, evolving from adversaries to romantic partners. She discloses her terminal leukemia, leading to emotional crises and deepened bonds, ultimately marrying Landon before her death. Her journey highlights faith, love, and acceptance in the face of mortality.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Mr. Kelly expels Landon for drinking, citing evidence from his car and a police report, despite Landon's claim he wasn't the one drinking; Landon reacts defiantly.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Expelled student
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Landon is expelled by Principal Kelly after Kelly presents empty bottles from Landon's car and a police blood alcohol report, despite Landon's denial that he was drinking.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Expelled from school and free from its authority, as stated by Kelly.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Aftermath of expulsion and the car accident consequences.
evidence_quotes: Mr. Kelly gestures for Landon to sit. Landon doesn't.
His forehead still shows a bruise.
MR. KELLY I am.
LANDON You expelling me?

relation_updates: Adversarial relationship with Principal Kelly solidified
status_updates: Expelled from school

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Landon reluctantly agrees to join the drama club as part of his re-enrollment, struggling with the demands but beginning to confront his self-perception and past.

timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Landon is conditionally re-enrolled with obligations to perform community service and participate in drama club.

timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Landon has acknowledged dependence on Jamie for help, and their relationship is set as adversarial yet cooperative.

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 173、INT. BEAUFORT HIGH - CORRIDOR - HALF HOUR LATER
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jamie reveals her fear and loneliness, feeling abandoned by faith; Landon comforts her and promises to help her live until she dies, reaffirming his support.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jamie and Landon watch Haley's Comet; overwhelmed, Landon proposes, asking Jamie to marry him, leaving her in emotional disbelief.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Landon proposes to Jamie while they watch Haley's Comet on the parsonage roof.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Landon asks Jamie to marry him after confirming her love and willingness.

HER FACE Illness, happiness, disbelief.

relation_updates: Landon and Jamie become engaged
status_updates: Landon is now engaged to Jamie

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Landon, moved by Reverend Sullivan's words that Jamie's miracle was him, embraces the reverend, finding closure as he prepares to remarry, honoring Jamie's wish.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Landon marries Jamie in a church ceremony and later visits her father, Reverend Sullivan, in the present day.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Landon marries Jamie in a ceremony where she is ill, and years later, he visits an elderly Reverend Sullivan, shown by their stiff embrace and Landon's fumbling words.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Landon is visiting Reverend Sullivan in the present day, with their interaction uncertain.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The reason for Landon's visit to Reverend Sullivan is unclear, as he fumbles his explanation.
evidence_quotes: Landon kneels to be the same height as Jamie in her chair.
Jamie takes Landon's hand in her right hand, her father's hand in her left.
Landon Carter, 33, waiting. Looking at a framed photo of Jamie as Lizzie in The Rainmaker...
The two men embrace. Stiff, uncomfortable, uncertain.

relation_updates: Landon becomes Jamie's husband
Landon maintains relationship with Reverend Sullivan
status_updates: Landon is married to Jamie
In present day, Landon is a 33-year-old widower
auxiliary.persona_anchor: A widower visiting his late wife's father.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Landon, moved by Reverend Sullivan's words that Jamie's miracle was him, embraces the reverend, finding closure as he prepares to remarry, honoring Jamie's wish.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Landon, a widower, returning his wedding ring to Reverend Sullivan before remarrying
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Landon gives his wedding ring to Reverend Sullivan, confirms his remarriage, and learns from the reverend that Jamie wanted him to remarry and considered him her miracle, leading to an emotional embrace.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Landon has returned his wedding ring and received confirmation of Jamie's wish, leaving him emotionally validated and ready to remarry
evidence_quotes: He gives his wedding ring to the Reverend.
REV. SULLIVAN Jamie wanted that. She told me.
REV. SULLIVAN She did. It was you.

relation_updates: Strengthened bond with Reverend Sullivan through emotional embrace and shared grief
status_updates: No longer wearing wedding ring, symbolizing transition from past marriage
auxiliary.persona_anchor: A man honoring Jamie's memory through gratitude and actions, as shown in his dialogue

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Landon Carter transforms from a reckless, popular high school student into a devoted partner and mature adult. After being expelled and forced into drama club, he develops a complex relationship with Jamie Sullivan that evolves from hostility to deep love. When Jamie reveals her terminal leukemia, Landon commits fully to supporting her, ultimately marrying her and finding purpose through their relationship.
